faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my East Tremont home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small holes in woolens, cashmere, or other natural fabrics, silky tubes or cases in closets, pale-colored moths flying near wardrobes, and larvae or webbing in stored clothing. You may also notice damage to carpets, upholstery, or fur items. Our East Tremont technicians provide thorough inspections to confirm the species and extent of infestation.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control cost in East Tremont?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in East Tremont ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of infestation, size of your home, and number of treatments required. Minor infestations in a single room may cost $550-$1,200, while severe whole-home infestations requiring multiple treatments and monitoring can reach $3,000-$5,000. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes for all East Tremont properties.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment results are often visible within 1-2 weeks, but complete elimination typically requires 4-8 weeks due to the moth life cycle. Multiple treatments spaced 2-3 weeks apart are usually necessary to target eggs, larvae, and adult moths. Our East Tremont team provides follow-up visits to ensure all life stages are eliminated and your fabrics remain protected.

faqs.question: Can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my East Tremont apartment?
faqs.answer: Yes! Prevention includes regularly cleaning woolens and natural fiber items, storing seasonal clothing in sealed containers, vacuuming closets and drawers frequently, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets, and maintaining low humidity levels. Our technicians provide East Tremont residents with customized prevention plans and can set up ongoing monitoring to catch any new activity before it becomes an infestation.
